title: papaver pavoninum subsp. ocellatum
intro: Papaver pavoninum subsp. ocellatum, also known as a subspecies of the Himalayan poppy, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloids, including protopine and berberine. The plant's health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat various skin conditions and wounds. Papaver pavoninum subsp. ocellatum is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat external wounds and skin conditions.
